verdict.answer_first: B states the fix in sentence 2: "split your dependency install from your source copy"; A also answers early but first adds caveat and a broad claim.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly: "The first thing to do" and "Do that first"; A gives the right fix but adds "Then, two quick wins" and diagnostic steps.
verdict.fabrication: A invents an unsupported statistic: "works for ~90%"; B says "usually eliminates most" without evidence but is less falsely precise.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives one ordered Dockerfile pattern and says to try it before other levers; A is useful but more spread out.
